title: 10 best herbal tinctures for hematuria
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for hematuria are liquid extracts of plants used to treat urinary bleeding, also known as hematuria. They help by reducing inflammation, improving kidney function, and promoting blood clotting, thereby alleviating symptoms. The best herbal tinctures for hematuria include Equisetum arvense, Juniperus communis, Astragalus membranaceus, Vaccinium macrocarpon, Sanguinaria canadensis, Taraxacum officinale, Urtica dioica, Hypericum perforatum, Cinchona officinalis, and Arctium lappa. If left untreated, hematuria can lead to chronic kidney damage, fatigue, and anemia, significantly impacting daily life, causing discomfort, pain, and decreased productivity.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bearberry tinctures helps with hematuria because it contains a unique compound called arbutin, which is converted to hydroquinone in the body. Hydroquinone has been shown to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing the frequency and severity of urinary tract infections that can cause hematuria. Additionally, bearberry's natural astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels and reduce inflammation, which can also contribute to its ability to alleviate hematuria symptoms. By targeting these underlying causes, bearberry tinctures can provide relief from hematuria and promote overall urinary health.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Bearberry tincture's astringent properties help to reduce inflammation and shrink the size of blood vessels, which can help to alleviate bleeding in the urinary tract.

Antioxidant activity: Bearberry tincture's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the kidneys and bladder tissues, contributing to hematuria.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Bearberry tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as arbutin and uva-ursi acid, help to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can alleviate symptoms of hematuria, including pain and burning during urination.
remedy_constituents: Ursolic acid: A triterpenoid compound that helps with hematuria by reducing inflammation in the urinary tract and exhibiting antimicrobial properties to prevent infections.

Gallic acid: A phenolic acid that helps with hematuria by acting as an antioxidant, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the urinary tract, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties to prevent infections.

Vitamin C (Ascorbic acid): A water-soluble vitamin that helps with hematuria by acting as an antioxidant, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the urinary tract, and promoting the healing of urinary tract tissues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain tannins and other compounds that have astringent properties, which can help reduce bleeding and inflammation in the urinary tract.
Fruits: The fruits of the plant contain anthocyanins and other antioxidants that may help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the urinary tract.

Barks: The bark of Arctostaphylos uva-ursi contains tannins and other compounds that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help prevent infections and promote healing in the urinary tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh arctostaphylos uva ursi leaves and stems in early summer when the plant is in full leaf.
Clean and dry the harvested material thoroughly to prevent contamination and promote tincture quality.
Combine 1 part of the dried arctostaphylos uva ursi material with 2 parts of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard the solids to obtain the final tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with hematuria because it has been traditionally used to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the urinary tract. The herb's demulcent properties create a protective barrier that helps to reduce inflammation and irritation, thereby reducing the bleeding associated with hematuria. Additionally, marshmallow's anti-inflammatory properties help to alleviate discomfort and pain, providing relief for those experiencing this condition. By targeting the root cause of the issue, herbal marshmallow tinctures can promote natural healing and recovery from hematuria.
remedy_properties: Demulcent properties: Marshmallow root's mucilages coat and soothe the urinary tract lining, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by hematuria, thereby promoting healing and comfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow root contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the urinary tract, alleviating symptoms of hematuria.

Antioxidant properties: Marshmallow root's antioxidants help protect the urinary tract from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of chronic inflammation and promoting overall kidney health.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages, a type of complex carbohydrate, help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the urinary tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with hematuria.

Flavonoids (notably kaempferol and quercetin): Flavonoids exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and alleviate the symptoms of hematuria, such as bleeding and pain.

Gallic acid: Gallic acid has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect the urinary tract from damage, thus alleviating symptoms of hematuria.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are a primary source of mucilage, which helps soothe and protect the urinary tract.
Leaves: Their mucilaginous properties aid in reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with hematuria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh or dried root of the plant with thick white roots.
Chop the roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for better extraction.
Combine the chopped roots with 80 proof vodka in a 1:5 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks to allow for extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the tincture.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Hydrastis canadensis are primarily used to make tinctures for hematuria because they contain high levels of berberine, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Hydrastis canadensis are occasionally used in tinctures for hematuria, as they also contain berberine and other compounds that may help to soothe and protect the urinary tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 3-4 ounces of fresh or dried hydrastis canadensis roots in the fall season or early spring.
Chop the roots into small pieces using a sharp knife or a root cutter tool to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped roots with 80-100 proof vodka or glycerin in a 1:5 ratio by weight in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar occasionally to enhance extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store in a cool dark place.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and 2 parts of vegetable glycerin.
Chop the roots into small pieces and combine with glycerin in a 1:2 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture in a clean glass jar for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place indefinitely.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh taraxacum officinale roots and leaves from a clean area on a sunny morning.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto tinctures helps with hematuria because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and irritation in the urinary tract. The herb's ability to block the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT) also contributes to its benefits, as DHT can exacerbate symptoms of benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH), a common cause of hematuria. remedy_properties: 5-alpha-reductase inhibition: Saw palmetto tincture helps reduce the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), which can contribute to enlarged prostate and benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH), common causes of hematuria.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Saw palmetto's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, alleviating symptoms such as burning or stinging sensations during urination that are associated with hematuria.

Pro-apoptotic properties: Some studies suggest that saw palmetto may promote the death of cancer cells, which can contribute to the development of hematuria; this property may also help reduce the risk of tumor growth and recurrence.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried serenoa repens root and stem material from a reputable supplier.
Combine the dried material with 500ml of 70 vodka in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container.
